commands.review_summary:
The PR preserves deferred MCP/LSP runtime selectors in inherited subagent tool allowlists, filters them through the effective policy layers, and adds focused regression coverage for both agent and gateway construction paths.

PR surface: Source +143, Tests +165. Total +308 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main replaces inherited allowlists with only materialized parent tool names, dropping deferred MCP/LSP selectors before child construction. The linked issue also provides a concrete exported-trajectory reproduction, though I did not execute it during this read-only review.

target.title: fix(agents): preserve runtime tool inheritance tokens
target.body:
## Summary
- Preserve runtime-materialized allowlist tokens when spawned sessions inherit an effective parent tool surface.
- Reuse the gateway explicit allowlist so restrictive subagent/tool policies can still let child attempts materialize bundled MCP/LSP runtimes before final policy filtering.
- Cover the shared helper, gateway resolution, and embedded tool construction paths with focused regression tests.

Refs #85030

## Verification
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/tool-policy.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts src/agents/agent-tools.ts src/agents/tool-policy.test.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.exclude.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-policy.test.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.exclude.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Spawned subagent inherited allowlists dropped runtime materialization policy tokens such as `bundle-mcp`, `group:plugins`, MCP `server__tool` names, and `lsp_` tool names when the parent effective tool surface did not already contain materialized runtime tools.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS with workspace dependencies installed via `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a terminal probe with `corepack pnpm exec tsx --eval` that loaded the patched `src/agents/tool-policy.ts` production module and printed the inherited allowlist produced from a parent surface containing `read`/`sessions_spawn` plus explicit runtime tool tokens. Captured after the patch on June 13, 2026 in the local checkout.
Evidence after fix: Terminal output from that production helper probe:

```json
{
  "inheritedToolAllowlist": [
    "read",
    "sessions_spawn",
    "bundle-mcp",
    "probe__search",
    "lsp_hover_typescript"
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: The copied output shows the inherited child allowlist keeps the concrete parent-visible tools plus the MCP/LSP runtime materialization tokens, while the ordinary unavailable `custom_plugin_tool` input is absent.
What was not tested: A live external MCP server/subagent end-to-end run with user credentials, and the full repository check suite.
target.author: yu-xin-c
